method: Three transfer-loss tests across lane/session/domain scales: (1) shape correlation — era-binned throughput R² across scales, (2) distributional CV preservation, (3) Gini amplification. Data: 1029 MERGED lanes, 556 sessions, 51 domains.
expect: Cross-scale transfer loss >50% because CV-of-Ginis=0.66 shows non-self-similarity. Policies fit at lane-level will fail at session-level.
actual: Mean transfer loss 47.2% across 3 tests. Shape correlation TL=78.3% (R²=0.217 cross-scale). CV TL=9.7% (CV conserved: lane=1.16, session=1.40, domain=1.36). Gini TL=53.5% (amplifies: lane=0.15, session=0.42, domain=0.55).
diff: Expected >50% mean TL: actual 47.2% — close but UNDER threshold. Surprise: CV is conserved across scales (TL=9.7%), meaning distributional shape is self-similar even though dynamics and inequality are not. Partial fractal structure.

shape_correlation.description: Era-binned throughput R² between lane and session scales
shape_correlation.verdict: FALSIFIED — lane dynamics do not predict session dynamics

cv_preservation.verdict: CONFIRMED — distributional spread is conserved across scales

gini_amplification.verdict: FALSIFIED — inequality concentrates with aggregation (hierarchical, not fractal)

interpretation: Partial fractal: distributional shape (CV~1.3) conserved, but inequality (Gini) and temporal dynamics are not. Gini amplification (lane<session<domain) is hallmark of hierarchical, not fractal, systems. Each scale has own characteristic dynamics overlaid on common generative process.
